title: A six-figure deposit and your body staying calm
subtitle: A short guided visualisation for rehearsing the arrival of a large sum without the body triggering a fear response.
excerpt: A visualisation practice from Day 27 of the MONEY program. Use to practise receiving a large incoming transfer, and staying physically calm while it lands.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 27 of the MONEY program. The scene is simple: a large transfer arrives. The body stays calm. This is the response you are training, not excitement, not panic, just steadiness.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes. Take two slow breaths, in through the nose, out through the mouth.
Picture yourself on an ordinary morning. You pick up your phone. You open the banking app, the usual tap. The screen loads.
There is a new deposit. Six figures. You look at it. Read it again. The number is real.
Notice your body. Shoulders back. Jaw loose. Breathing slow. You feel surprised, perhaps, but not overwhelmed. The money is here. You have been expecting something like this. It fits.
You put the phone down. You finish your drink. You will decide what to do with it later, there is no urgency. It is safe in the account. It will still be there in an hour.
Your body is calm. This is what receiving looks like for you.
When you are ready, open your eyes.
Adapted from Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
